message.content:
Research and find better visual reference sources for a railway maintenance scheduling system.

**What we're building:**
- Planning system for 128 trains over 12 years
- Mileage-based preventive maintenance (A-Check 10k km, B-Check 50k km, C-Check 200k km, D-Overhaul 800k km)
- Semantic zoom: MACRO (heatmap), MESO (Gantt blocks), MICRO (detailed cards)
- Sandbox mode for what-if scenarios
- Dark mode enterprise app

**What we need references for:**
1. Gantt charts with LOD (Level of Detail) - zoom in/out changes visualization
2. What-if scenario planning UI patterns
3. Fleet/asset management dashboards (railway or aviation)
4. Resource utilization heatmaps
5. Mileage tracking interfaces

**Search for:**
- Real software screenshots (not marketing pages)
- Figma/Dribbble concepts for enterprise scheduling
- Aviation MRO (Maintenance Repair Overhaul) software interfaces
- Railway asset management software screenshots
- Enterprise planning tools with scenario comparison

Provide URLs to relevant images or articles with screenshots that would actually help implementation.

## Gantt Chart Level of Detail (LOD) and Zoom Visualization

Several project management and visualization tools offer level of detail (LOD) features for Gantt charts that allow users to zoom and adjust the granularity of timeline views:

### Key Concepts

Level of detail (LOD) is a scale level defined in a tiling scheme. It represents the zoom level value for a map view or a scene view. While this definition comes from mapping applications, the concept applies similarly to Gantt charts.

### Multi-Level Timeline Features

**Inforiver Analytics+:**
Zoom in and out on the Gantt timeline for high-level project overviews and detailed task status tracking. The Analytics+ Gantt supports detailed planning and tracking with the Zoom option. You can even customize the degree of zoom and tailor it to your data.(no content)You can configure up to 5 levels of zoom.(no content)For instance, the highest level could be Year/Qtr/Month and the lowest could be Day/Hour/Minute - depending on the timelines defined in your project.

**xViz Gantt Chart for Power BI:**
The xViz Gantt Chart offers the capability to incorporate up to three distinct levels within the timeline, thereby enhancing the provision of additional date and time context while maintaining a clutter-free timeline view.(no content)Upon further zooming in, the levels transition to Month -> Day -> Hour, and with even more granularity, they progress to Day -> Hour -> Minute, accommodating the user's need for varying levels of temporal detail.

**Syncfusion Gantt Chart:**
The zooming feature enables you to view the tasks in the project clearly from minute to year timespan.(no content)ZoomIn - To perform zoom in action on Gantt timeline. ZoomOut - To perform zoom out action on Gantt timeline. ZoomToFit - To show all tasks with timeline fit into available chart width.

**Productive:**
Adjust the level of detail you see by zooming in or out (click on the magnifying glass in the upper right). The Today button allows you to quickly jump back to the current date on your timeline.

**GoodDay:**
You can fold any subproject or task when you need to get a high level view or expand to see all sub-items. Select one of the available zoom options, e.g. day/week/month, or use the fit-to-screen option to automatically adjust zoom to display the entire chart.

### Best Practices

Choose an appropriate time scale (days, weeks, months, or quarters) that best represents your project timeline. Ensure that all crucial project milestones and deadlines are visible within the chosen time frame.(no content)For example, you can zoom out to a bigger picture of your project's tasks by displaying Years and Months, or you can zoom in to see the exact start and finish dates for your project's tasks by changing the timescale to Weeks and Days.

Here are the search results for aviation MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul) software interfaces:

## Overview of Aviation MRO Software

Aviation MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul) software is a specialized digital solution designed to manage and optimize the various processes involved in maintaining aircraft. It provides a suite of tools for planning, scheduling, executing maintenance tasks, managing inventory, and ensuring regulatory compliance. By integrating these functions, MRO software streamlines operations, reduces downtime, and enhances overall operational efficiency.

## Key Software Solutions

### Ramco Aviation MRO
Ramco MRO Aviation Solution is a next-generation aviation software that addresses end-to-end Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul requirements for all types of MROs - Line, Component, Engine, Hangar, Heavy maintenance, and OEM aftermarket services. Ramco's technology leads the industry in digitizing MRO operations, enhancing turnaround times (TAT), improving customer satisfaction, and service level compliance.(no content)The solution offers established APIs with Aeroxchange, FedEx, SAP, and other 3rd party systems, providing enhanced productivity through Mobility, Chatbots, Hubs, and event-based workflow.

### JSSI Traxxall MRO Manager
Introduced in fall 2021, JSSI Traxxall's cloud-based MRO Manager is simple, efficient, right-sized, and based on a subscription model. It provides comprehensive visibility including the status of ongoing projects, resource allocation, labor costs, and invoice generation.(no content)The system may be configured to interoperate with existing automated systems including accounting and flight scheduling.

### SOMA Software
SOMA Software is designed specifically to address MRO challenges, providing a centralized platform for real-time, automated aviation maintenance management. Operators can visualize the status of every aircraft in the fleet, with smart alerts that highlight maintenance issues before they escalate. SOMA lets you automate maintenance planning based on flight hours, calendar days, or cycles.

### Smart 145
Based on the cloud, Smart 145 is an all-in-one ERP solution designed to optimize the workflows of aviation repair stations and part suppliers. It simplifies and improves daily operations by managing everything from a single, intuitive platform.

### IFS MRO Software
From contract management and quoting to final invoicing, IFS MRO software supports the full lifecycle of a visit, reducing turnaround time without compromising on regulatory requirements or safety.

### ASA Software (SAM)
SAM is built to resemble the Windows Office user interface, ensuring familiarity and accessibility. With SAM, you only pay for how many concurrent users you want using the software, not per aircraft. It seamlessly connects CAMO and MRO together in a powerful maintenance solution.

### AvPro Software
AvPro is a modular suite of aircraft & component maintenance applications designed to work independently or to be seamlessly integrated with each other. It provides an all-in-one digital platform to manage MRO tasks, fleet operations, and compliance. By combining work orders, inventory, accounting, and reporting in one system, it reduces paperwork, improves efficiency, and ensures safety and regulatory standards.

## Key Features of MRO Software

Aviation MRO software solutions streamline maintenance, repair, and overhaul operations with features such as work order management, inventory tracking, compliance monitoring, predictive maintenance, scheduling, and real-time analytics, ensuring effectiveness, regulatory compliance, and minimizing downtime.(no content)Cloud-based MRO software can seamlessly integrate with other aviation systems, including ERP, IoT, AI analytics, and flight management systems.

Based on my search, here's an overview of what-if scenario planning UI enterprise software options:

## Enterprise What-If Scenario Planning Software

### Top Enterprise Solutions

1. **Epicflow**
   - What-If Analysis is an AI-driven feature of Epicflow scenario planning software that shows you how project changes will affect your project environment in the future.
   - By enabling this feature, you switch to a special mode where you can test management decisions without applying them to your real projects. If there are any project change requests or a necessity for project environment alterations (e.g., a request to initiate an extra project, illness of a critical resource, changed project requirements), you can try different scenarios in a simulated environment and choose the best one.

2. **Anaplan**
   - Anaplan is a cloud-based enterprise planning platform built for large organizations that require cross-functional modeling and strategic scenario planning. It's known for its flexibility and massive scale, connecting finance, operations, and supply chain into one dynamic model.
   - Users can simulate complex "what-if" situations across business units, products, or regions, and instantly see the financial impact. Its powerful modeling engine allows deep driver-based forecasts, but only after a steep setup and configuration process.

3. **Planisware**
   - Planisware 5 has complete "What-If" capabilities that enable users to create and compare different project scenarios, as well as simulate portfolio changes.

4. **Board International**
   - Board International's scenario planning software combines tech-powered planning and forecasting to model complex what-if scenarios. It supports driver-based planning across finance, supply chain, and sales, with real-time data integration and multidimensional analysis.

5. **Pigment**
   - Pigment's scenario planning software features what-if modeling and visualizations such as tornado and waterfall charts. Its prompted modeling capabilities suggest new scenarios for users to explore. The platform's real-time data integration from ERP and CRM systems enhances cross-team collaboration.

6. **IFS (WISE)**
   - The what-if scenario explorer (WISE) featured in IFS Planning & Scheduling Optimization means that your ideas can be thoroughly tested before you put them into practice. Using a wide range of 'what-if scenarios' you can enter any situation with the backing of a highly accurate prediction of the consequence of your actions. By testing before committing to a course of action you minimize your risk and maximize your profits.

### Mid-Market Solutions

7. **Cube**
   - Cube lets you model any scenario on the fly—no chaos, no consultants. Build, compare, and analyze unlimited what-ifs right inside Cube and across Excel, Google Sheets, and everywhere you work.
   - What-if modeling, rolling forecasts, and assumption adjustments within a familiar spreadsheet interface. Cube is best suited for small to mid-sized companies that have outgrown spreadsheets but don't need the complexity of enterprise-grade tools.

8. **Vena Solutions**
   - Vena combines a cloud database with an Excel front end, preserving spreadsheet flexibility while adding centralized data, workflow, and controls. Its scenario planning provides driver based models, rapid what ifs, and side by side comparisons, all in the familiar Excel interface. For teams standardizing on Microsoft 365, Vena offers a pragmatic middle ground between spreadsheets and enterprise planning platforms.

### Key Features to Look For

Real-time simulation is critical—when market conditions shift, finance needs to see the P&L impact immediately, not days later. The right platform connects FP&A with HR, supply chain, and sales so every plan is aligned around shared drivers. Scenario planning should be transparent but controlled; tools with workflows, versioning, and role-based access keep teams coordinated without compromising data integrity.(no content)The best scenario planning tools don't just make your model faster. They make your team smarter. They help you spot risks before they hit. They let you run a scenario live in a meeting, and shift direction before the quarter ends.

### Official Figma Templates
Figma offers free Gantt chart templates to keep your team and project on schedule. Figma's online Gantt chart template makes it possible to see who's working on what, plan timelines, and visually track progress with your team.

### FigJam Gantt Chart Maker
The Gantt chart is a type of bar chart used for project planning that typically lists tasks and responsibilities on its vertical axis and time intervals on its horizontal axis. The chart stretches from the start date of a project to its completion, with horizontal bars that correspond with each task and activity and its duration.

### Community Templates
Several community-created Gantt chart templates are available:

3. **Resizable Gantt Chart** - Organizes tasks over time; designed for project timelines, task scheduling, or milestone tracking. This component is easy to customize, resize and is 100% auto layout.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about semantic zoom and Google Earth-style interfaces for desktop apps:

## Semantic Zoom

Semantic zoom lets the user switch between two different views of the same content so that they can quickly navigate through a large set of grouped data. According to Microsoft's documentation:

- The zoomed-in view is the main view of the content, where you show individual data items.
- The zoomed-out view is a higher-level view of the same content. You typically show the group headers for a grouped data set in this view.
- For example, when viewing an address book, the user could zoom out to quickly jump to the letter "W", and zoom in on that letter to see the names associated with it.(no content)Don't confuse semantic zooming with optical zooming. While they share both the same interaction and basic behavior, optical zoom refers to the adjustment of magnification for a content area or object such as a photograph.

## Historical Context - Archy/Raskin's Vision

Raskin proposed an alternative to the reigning windows and folders paradigm, in which your entire OS is a single sheet, a kind of landscape in which you can zoom out to a map view or zoom down into specific words and images, a kind of "google maps for your digital space." This concept was explored in the Archy software project.

## Google Earth-Style Interfaces

Google Earth Studio is free to use and works with the desktop version of Google Chrome.(no content)In Earth Studio, you can zoom in and out by clicking and dragging with the right mouse button, or by scrolling. You can hold ⌥ while dragging to orbit around the center of your viewport, giving fine-tuned control of your camera's heading and tilt.

For Windows desktop apps, the SemanticZoom control doesn't have any visual representation of its own. It's a host control that manages the transition between 2 other controls that provide the views of your content, typically ListView or GridView controls.

Here are the search results for "heatmap density visualization resource utilization dashboard":

## Heatmap Density Visualization for Resource Utilization Dashboards

### Overview

A heat map visualizes the density of data points across values returned by a query. Heat maps depict several ranges of values, referred to as buckets, as colored segments for each time recorded in data points. The color of each bucket corresponds to the number of data points whose values are inside of that bucket's range. Colors typically fall in a gradient where the number of data points in a bucket correspond to that bucket color's intensity in the heat map.

### Resource Heatmaps

A resource heatmap is a visual representation of how effectively resources are allocated against their capacity or availability. It typically uses color gradients to depict different levels of utilization across various dimensions such as role, team, department, location, etc. The heatmap enables users to identify and address instances of over- and underutilization and maintain optimum productivity.

### Types of Resource Heatmaps

1. **Overall Resource Heatmap**: An overall resource heatmap offers a high-level view of resource utilization across departments, teams, and projects. It highlights overall workload distribution, making it easier to swiftly identify overused or underutilized resources. This enables managers to make informed decisions to balance workloads & enhance the resource health index.

2. **Strategic Heatmap**: A strategic heatmap visually represents the amount of time resources spend toward high-priority, strategic initiatives. It measures their utilization levels when engaged in these key activities, which are crucial for achieving the organization's long-term objectives.

3. **Billable Resource Heatmap**: A billable resource heatmap illustrates how much time resources spend on revenue-generating projects against their capacity or availability. It offers clear visibility into billable hours, that is, the time spent on activities that can be directly invoiced to the client.

### Color Coding Standards

Color codes typically represent:
- Red – Overutilized, carrying excessive workload
- Pink – Underutilized, not fully engaged
- Dark Grey – Averagely utilized, workload is balanced
- Green/Dark Green – Optimally utilized, working at peak efficiency
- Light Blue – Not utilized and are available for tasks

### Dashboard Tools and Platforms

**Veeam ONE**: The Heatmap dashboard visually represents key resource utilization in your backup infrastructure. The dashboard allows real-time monitoring of free space on repositories as well as repository and proxy usage.

**Grafana**: Grafana heatmaps visualize a large density of your data distribution and condense large amounts of data through various color schemes that are easier to interpret.

**Chronosphere**: This platform generates heatmaps showing CPU utilization for pods, with buckets generated based on the distribution of returned series. The graph renders a count of how many pods have utilization grouped into a given bucket. The heatmap helps identify what typical utilization looks like and how much variance there is across pods.

### Benefits of Heatmap Visualization

A single heatmap can effectively display thousands of data points in a compact, comprehensible format. The color gradient provides a natural way to understand relative values: darker or brighter usually means "more."(no content)Resource heatmaps support long-term workforce planning. By analyzing utilization trends, managers can forecast capacity needs, make data-driven staffing decisions, and prevent resource bottlenecks before they impact project timelines. This way, firms can streamline operations, improve project outcomes, and maximize team performance.

### Use Cases

Density heatmaps can aid in the allocation of resources based on the density of certain events or occurrences. For instance, in retail, you can analyze customer density heatmaps to determine the ideal locations for opening new stores or adjusting staffing levels.

### 6. **Heatmap & Resource Utilization Visualization**

**Heatmap Documentation & Examples:**
- [Grafana Heatmap Visualization](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/visualizations/heatmap/) - Data density visualization with color gradients

**Resource Planning Heatmaps:**
- [Saviom: Resource Heatmap Analysis Guide](https://www.saviom.com/blog/how-does-resource-heatmap-optimize-profitable-utilization/) - Over/underutilization color coding (red=overuse, green=optimal, blue=available)
- [Planview AdaptiveWork Resource Heat Map](https://www.planview.com/products-solutions/products/adaptivework/resource-heat-map/) - Strategic resource visualization
- [ServiceNow Heatmap Visualization Examples](https://www.servicenow.com/docs/bundle/yokohama-now-intelligence/page/use/par-for-workspace/task/dv-example-heatmap.html)

**Design Principles (Macro Layer reference):**
- Color gradients: Yellow (low) → Orange (medium) → Red (high saturation)
- Supports thousands of data points in compact format
- Enables identification of capacity bottlenecks at a glance

## Specific Design Recommendations for Your System

Based on these references, here's what to prioritize:

| Element | Recommended Source | Why |
|---------|-------------------|-----|
| **Gantt LOD** | Inforiver (5-level zoom) or xViz (3-level) | Proven timeline transitions |
| **Heatmap** | Grafana/Chronosphere patterns | Production-grade density viz |
| **Dashboard Layout** | Fleetio, Fleet Chaser mockups | Real fleet management UX |
| **Scenario UI** | Epicflow pattern | "Simulated environment" metaphor fits perfectly |
| **MRO Precedent** | Ramco or SOMA | Closest operational parallel to railways |
| **Dark Mode Styling** | Hope UI or SAKAI template | Enterprise-appropriate |
| **Semantic Zoom Pattern** | Microsoft + Google Earth Studio | Well-documented interaction model |
